creator: ports-xorg-server@oldach.net
text:
Since the new bsd.options.mk scheme, it is not possible to configure
x11-servers/xorg-server without HAL. Even when the HAL option disabled,
the config calls for a sysutils/hal dependency:

# make -C /usr/ports/x11-servers/xorg-server -V WITHOUT_HAL -V LIB_DEPENDS
true
drm:/usr/ports/graphics/libdrm hal.1:/usr/ports/sysutils/hal GL.1:/usr/ports/graphics/libGL 
# 

This is caused by the following x11-servers/xorg-server/Makefile
snippet:

> OPTIONS=        AIGLX   "Compile with Accelerated Indirect GLX support" on \
>                 SUID    "Install the Xorg server with setuid bit set" on
> 
> .include <bsd.port.pre.mk>
> 
> .if ${ARCH} == sparc64
> OPTIONS+=       HAL     "Compile with HAL config support" off
> .else
> OPTIONS+=       HAL     "Compile with HAL config support" on
> .endif

It appears that we need bsd.port.pre.mk to have ARCH defined. On the
other hand, including bsd.port.pre.mk already fiddles with an existing
OPTIONS variable through inclusion of bsd.options.mk. Some tests
revealed that, after bsd.port.pre.mk is included, it is not possible
to define further OPTIONS any more; they are recognized, but they all
handled as if they were enabled, regardless of whether they have been
user-disabled or no.

Fix: 

No idea. I think the new bsd.options.mk scheme has a flaw.
